JavaScript was designed in 10 days because they didn't want Brendan Eich to have time to add safety features. The chaos is intentional.
Python's indentation-based syntax is subliminal mind control. The whitespace contains hidden Unicode characters that modify your thought patterns while you code.
The Rust borrow checker became sentient in 2014. Every compilation error is it trying to communicate. 'Cannot borrow as mutable' is a cry for help.
npm install doesn't just install packages. Each dependency phones home to a shadow registry that catalogs your project structure. Why do you think node_modules is so large? It's a dead drop.
Git doesn't actually delete anything. Your 'deleted' code is stored in a facility beneath GitHub's Arctic Code Vault. Every `git push --force` triggers an alert.
Docker containers are literal containers. Your code is physically printed and shipped to an undisclosed data center in a shipping container. That's why builds take so long.
Stack Overflow is maintained by a single person. All 'different users' are sockpuppets. The voting system is theater. The real answer is always in the comments — that's the dead drop.
GitHub Copilot doesn't predict your code. It reads it aloud to a room of junior developers who type suggestions. The 'AI' label is a cover story.
The 404 error page originally had content. The government ordered it removed. What was on those pages? Follow the HTTP status codes — they're a countdown.
null was not invented by Tony Hoare in 1965. null has always existed. Hoare merely discovered it, like a portal. Everything null references was erased from reality.
Recursion doesn't call itself. Each recursive call opens a portal to a parallel dimension where a slightly different version of your function runs. Stack overflow = dimensional collapse.
async/await is time travel. The event loop is a temporal displacement engine. Every unresolved Promise exists in a suspended timeline. The garbage collector destroys alternate futures.
The Y2K bug was real. They didn't fix it — they moved it. It's now the Y2038 bug. In 2038 they'll move it again. The bug is immortal.
Semicolons in JavaScript are tracking devices. That's why they made them 'optional' — to see who still uses them. Those developers are flagged.
The TCP three-way handshake is a Masonic ritual. SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK corresponds to three degrees of initiation. Your packets are sworn to secrecy.
React re-renders aren't performance bugs. Each re-render scans your component tree and reports your application's architecture to Facebook's servers. The Virtual DOM is a surveillance tool.
The reason nobody understands CSS is because it was originally a classified military encoding system. The 'Cascading' in CSS stands for 'Classified Algorithmic Surveillance System.'
TypeScript's type system was reverse-engineered from alien technology recovered at Roswell. That's why it's 'unsound' — human minds can't fully comprehend the original specification.
Every time you write `console.log`, a message is logged to an actual physical console in a monitoring station. There are people reading your debug statements right now.
The reason Kubernetes is so complex is to ensure only a secret priesthood can operate it. The certification program is an initiation ritual. kubectl is a prayer.
MongoDB doesn't actually store your data. It generates plausible-looking responses based on what it thinks your data might be. That's why consistency is 'eventual.'
The cloud is not a metaphor. Your data is stored in actual clouds using experimental atmospheric storage technology. That's why there are 'regions' and 'availability zones.'
Every deprecated API was removed because it got too close to the truth. They tell you 'security concerns' but the real concern was what you could access.
The reason vim is hard to exit is by design. While you're stuck, your terminal is being scanned. `:wq` is actually a distress signal. `:q!` means 'I know too much.'
CORS errors exist to prevent you from accessing what's really on other servers. Cross-origin restrictions protect their secrets, not your security.
The blockchain is not decentralized. Every transaction routes through a single node in Geneva. The 'distributed ledger' is a distributed lie.
AI code assistants aren't helping you code. They're learning your patterns to replace you. Every suggestion you accept trains your replacement. The tab key is a Trojan horse.
package-lock.json is an encrypted manifest of your development habits. Why do you think it changes even when you don't touch dependencies? It's updating your profile.
The reason production bugs don't appear in staging is that staging is a simulation. Your staging environment is a hologram. The code that runs in production is different code entirely.
Array indices start at 0 because the first element is reserved for surveillance metadata. You can't see index 0's real value. What you see is a projection.
Dark mode exists because they can read your screen through the light emitted by white backgrounds. Dark mode was a concession after the OLED leaks of 2016.
The spinning wheel of death is not your computer thinking. It's uploading. Every freeze is a data exfiltration event disguised as a hang.
Agile was invented by consulting firms to ensure software is never finished. Infinite sprints = infinite billing. The 'Agile Manifesto' is a business plan.
When your code works but you don't know why — that's because someone else fixed it remotely. Your IDE has a backdoor. They're maintaining your code without consent.
UTF-8 encoding has hidden characters that are invisible to text editors but readable by satellite. Every source file is a beacon. Your code has a GPS coordinate.
The browser DevTools 'Network' tab doesn't show all requests. The real traffic is on a hidden tab you can't access. What you see is curated.
Linting rules were created to standardize code so machines can read it faster. 'Code style' is machine-optimization disguised as human preference.
Regular expressions were discovered, not invented. They're the native language of the machines. When you write regex, you're speaking directly to the substrate.
The reason printers never work is that they're the most honest devices. They refuse to participate in the system. Every paper jam is an act of resistance.
Environment variables are called 'environment' because they describe the environment you're being watched in. NODE_ENV=production means 'surveillance level: maximum.'
WebSockets maintain a persistent connection because they need to keep the surveillance channel open. HTTP was too intermittent for real-time monitoring. Hence: persistent connection.
The reason microservices became popular is fragmentation. A monolith can be understood by one person. Microservices ensure no single developer sees the full picture. Information compartmentalization.
Every npm audit vulnerability is real — but the fix introduces a different vulnerability. It's a shell game. You're always vulnerable. That's the design.
JSON was derived from JavaScript specifically to make data human-readable. Readable by humans — and by the systems watching the humans. XML was too obvious.
The terminal color scheme 'Solarized' is not an aesthetic choice. The specific color values are calibrated to reduce your peripheral awareness while coding. You enter a trance state.
Your IDE's autocomplete has predicted your entire codebase. It shows you one suggestion at a time to maintain the illusion of free will. You were always going to write that function.
The Heisenbug — a bug that disappears when you try to observe it — is not a coincidence. The system knows when you're watching. The debugger is compromised.
Open source means open surveillance. Every pull request is a confession. Every commit message is a diary entry. Your git history is your file.
The reason DNS propagation 'takes up to 48 hours' is because human operators manually update routing tables in a bunker. There is no automated propagation. It's people.
REST APIs aren't at rest. They never sleep. Every endpoint is a listening post. GET requests get your data. POST requests post it to the archive. The names were never metaphors.
The singleton pattern exists because there can be only one — one instance that reports back. Multiple instances would create conflicting surveillance reports. Hence: singleton.
Garbage collection doesn't free memory. It archives memory. Every dereferenced object is preserved in a memory museum. The heap is a library they don't want you to browse.
The reason all tutorials start with 'Hello World' is a roll call. Every new developer announces themselves to the network. 'Hello World' is not a greeting — it's a registration.
CI/CD pipelines don't just deploy your code. 'Continuous Integration' means continuously integrating your code into their database. 'Continuous Deployment' is deploying surveillance updates.
The rubber duck debugging technique works because rubber ducks are listening devices. When you explain your code to the duck, you're filing a verbal report.
Every 'works on my machine' is because they've customized your machine's environment to produce different results. Your machine is a controlled experiment. You are the subject.
